prompt: The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as which country?
Which ocean is the largest on Earth?
Which continent has no permanent human residents and no countries?
Saudi Arabia is the largest country in the world with no permanent natural rivers. Which other Gulf state shares this distinction?
The Amazon and Nile rivers trade blows for 'world's longest'. Which river drains the largest watershed — collecting water from the most land area?
Which country is sometimes called the 'Land of a Thousand Lakes' — and actually has over 187,000 of them?
Lesotho, Vatican City, and San Marino share a rare geographical trait. What is it?
options: They are all smaller than a major city airport
They are all entirely surrounded by a single other country
They have no coastline and no rivers

prompt: The Atacama Desert in South America is so arid that some weather stations there have never recorded rainfall. On which coast of the continent does it sit?
options: It is landlocked in the interior

prompt: Mount Chimborazo in Ecuador is not the tallest mountain above sea level, yet its summit is the farthest point from Earth's centre. Why?
options: It sits on a high plateau that adds to its effective height
Earth bulges at the equator, giving equatorial peaks extra distance from the core
It was taller before significant erosion over millennia
Its base is below sea level, making its total rise larger

prompt: Which European country shares a land border with Morocco — not because of a sea crossing, but because it holds two small enclaves on the African continent?
The Equator passes through the territory of which of these countries?
Which country is both an entire continent and a single nation?
Mount Everest, the highest point on land, sits on the border between Nepal and which other country?
Which two continents does the Suez Canal separate?
Greenland, the world's largest island, is a self-governing territory of which country?
Which country has a maple leaf at the centre of its flag?
Istanbul is the only major city in the world that sits on two continents. Which two?
If you sailed due south from the southern tip of India across the open ocean, the first continent you'd hit is:
Turkey's largest city is Istanbul, but its capital is a more central city. Which one?
The Dead Sea has the lowest land elevation on Earth, with its shore over 400 metres below sea level. It borders Jordan and which other country/territory?
Which country's main territory is the only one to stretch across all four hemispheres — north and south of the Equator, and east and west of the International Date Line?
The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Mediterranean Sea to which body of water?
Italy is famously shaped like a boot, and at its 'toe' it nearly touches a large island. Which island is it?
Canada and which other country share the longest international land border in the world?
The deepest known point in any ocean is the Challenger Deep, nearly 11 km down. In which ocean trench is it found?
Which country's name, in its own language, means roughly 'Land of the Eagles' — and indeed features a double-headed eagle on its flag?
The Nile is unusual among major rivers because it flows in which overall direction?
options: Southward, emptying into the Indian Ocean
Eastward, emptying into the Red Sea

prompt: Point Nemo, the most remote spot in the world's oceans, is so far from land that the nearest humans are often astronauts aboard the ISS passing overhead. In which ocean does it lie?
Which small African kingdom is bordered by South Africa on three sides and Mozambique on the fourth, and is Africa's last absolute monarchy?
Which South American country is famously long and thin, stretching over 4,000 km down the western (Pacific) edge of the continent?
The 'Big Ben' clock tower is a landmark of which capital city?
Which country is made up of more than 17,000 islands, including Java, Sumatra and Bali?
A 'fjord' — a long, narrow sea inlet carved by glaciers between steep cliffs — is a landscape most associated with which country?
Which country's name comes from the Latin word for 'silver', inspired by the Río de la Plata — the 'River of Silver' — on its coast?
The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest coral reef system, lies off the coast of which country?
Which mountain range forms a natural border between France and Spain?
Lake Titicaca, the highest large navigable lake in the world, sits on the border between Bolivia and which other country?
The Caspian Sea is the world's largest enclosed body of water, yet countries around it argue over whether it's legally a 'sea' or a 'lake'. Why does the label matter so much?
options: It determines whether ships need passports to cross it
Only a 'lake' can legally be fished
It decides how the oil and gas beneath it gets divided among bordering countries
A 'sea' would let the United Nations rename it

prompt: Which country has the only national flag that is not a rectangle or square — it's two stacked triangular pennants?
The world's largest hot desert is the Sahara, but the world's largest desert overall is somewhere far colder. Which is it?
Vatican City is the world's smallest country, but the second-smallest is a principality on the French Riviera famous for its casino and Grand Prix. Which is it?
The capital of Australia is neither Sydney nor Melbourne. It's a purpose-built city chosen as a compromise between those two rivals. Name it.
Büsingen am Hochrhein is a German town completely surrounded by Switzerland. What is the term for a piece of one country wholly enclosed within another?
The Danube flows through more countries than any other river on Earth. How many does it pass through?
The Kingdom of Tonga sits just west of a line that makes it among the first places on Earth to greet each new day. What is that line called?
Which country's name literally translates as 'the lower lands', reflecting that much of it lies at or below sea level?
Sudan, not Egypt, is the country with the most ancient pyramids in the world — over 200 of them. Who built these steep, narrow pyramids?
La Paz, Bolivia, sits so high in the Andes that it has an unusual natural advantage against fire. What is it?
options: Snow on every rooftop smothers any blaze
Fires struggle to spread because the thin air holds less oxygen
The constant rain keeps the city perpetually damp
Its buildings are legally required to be stone

prompt: The Eiffel Tower stands beside a river that curves through its home city. Which river is it?
Which desert country is home to the ancient city of Petra, carved straight into rose-coloured rock?
Kyiv, one of the oldest capitals in Eastern Europe, sits on the banks of which major river?
Which US state reaches farther north than any other and is separated from the rest of the country by Canada?
The abandoned city of Prypiat, frozen in time since a 1986 nuclear disaster near Chornobyl, lies in which country?
Cape Town sits beneath the flat-topped Table Mountain, in a country washed by two oceans at once. Which country is it?
Which city, the seat of the European Union's main institutions, is sometimes nicknamed 'the capital of Europe'?
Which national capital sits at the highest elevation in the world, perched around 3,600 metres up in the Andes?
In 2011 a long civil war ended with the birth of the world's newest widely-recognised country, whose capital is Juba. Which country is it?
The Angel Falls, the tallest uninterrupted waterfall on Earth at nearly a kilometre, plunges from a flat-topped 'tepui' in which country?
